title: Browse Mode (Premium)
description: Browse Mode lets you view all your cards in carousel format without affecting your study progress. Perfect for quick reviews, previewing content, or sharing cards with others.
content:
## Browse Mode vs. Study Mode

Browse Mode is a **view-only** version of Study Mode:

| Feature | Study Mode | Browse Mode |
|---------|-----------|-------------|
| **Cards shown** | Only due cards | All cards in deck |
| **Grading buttons** | ✅ Yes (Easy, Good, Hard, Again) | ❌ No |
| **View term & definition** | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes |
| **Swipe between cards** | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes |
| **Edit/delete cards** | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes |
| **Interactive gestures** | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes |

**Use Browse Mode when you want to:**

* Review all cards without studying
* Preview a deck's content
* Check what's in a deck without changing progress

***

## Starting Browse Mode

From the home screen:

**Tap a deck** → **"Browse Cards"**

All cards appear in carousel view.

***

## The Browse Screen

### Card Layout

```
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│      [← Back]        [⋮]    │ ← Top bar
├─────────────────────────────┤
│                             │
│        TERM (Question)      │ ← Front of card
│                             │
├─────────────────────────────┤ ← Drag to resize
│                             │
│     DEFINITION (Answer)     │ ← Back of card
│                             │
│        (always visible)     │
└─────────────────────────────┘
```

***

## Related Topics

* [Study Mode](/docs/study/) - Study cards with spaced repetition
* [Editing Cards](/docs/cards-list/editing/) - Modify card content
* [Cards List](/docs/cards-list/) - View cards in list format
* [Deck Settings](/docs/settings/deck-settings/) - Configure deck behavior

title: Deleting Cards
description: Delete cards that are duplicates, outdated, or no longer needed in your study deck.
content:
## Deleting Single Cards ### Method 1: Swipe to Delete (Fastest) **Swipe a card left or right** to quickly delete it. ### Method 2: Card Menu **Tap a card** -> Select **"Delete"** ## Deleting Multiple Cards For bulk deletion, use [Multi-Select Mode](/docs/cards-list/multi-select/):
*** ## Related Topics * [Multi-Select Operations](/docs/cards-list/multi-select/) – Bulk operations including deletion * [Editing Cards](/docs/cards-list/editing/) – Modify cards instead of deleting * [Database Backup](/docs/home/database-backup/) – Protect against accidental deletion * \[Sync

title: Edge Bars (Left or Right)
description: Visual indicators on the left or right side of cards that show learning progress or recent sync activity.
content:
### Show learning status

Shows your memorization progress for each card:

* **⚪ Gray** - Disabled card (won't appear in study mode)
* **🟠 Orange** - Forgotten card
* **🟢 Green** - Memorized card

### Show recently synced

Shows which cards changed during the last sync with your Excel or CSV file:

#### From File to App

* **🟠 Bright Orange** - Card was updated from the file
* **🟢 Bright Green** - Card was added from the file

#### From App to File

* **🟠 Dim Orange** - Card was updated from the app
* **🟢 Dim Green** - Card was added from the app

#### Unchanged

* **No color** - Card wasn't modified during sync

Learn more about how sync works in [Sync Cards](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards).

### Changing Edge Bar Settings

Configure edge bar visibility in [App Settings](/docs/settings/all-decks#show-left-edge-bar).

title: Cards List - Managing Your Flashcards
description: The Cards List screen is your deck management hub. View all cards at once, search, reorder, edit, delete.
content:
### Opening Cards List From the home screen: 1. **Tap "⋮" on a deck** to open the deck menu. 2. **Select "List Cards"** You'll see all your cards in a scrollable list/grid view. *** ### Card List Display Each card shows: * **#** - Card number, ordinal position * **Term** - front of card * **Definition** - back of card * **Edge Bars** (left or right) - Colored bars indicate card status * **Max lines** - Configurable in [Deck Settings](/docs/settings/deck-settings/)
*** ### Toolbar Actions * **🔍 Search** (Premium feature)- Filter cards by term or definition text * **+ Add Card** - Add a new card at the of deck * **⋮ More Menu:** * Sync with Excel/CSV * Export to Excel * Export to CSV * ⚙ Settings *** ## Card Actions ### Tap a Card **Tap a Card** opens the card's **action menu:** * **Edit** - Modify term/definition (see [Editing Cards](/docs/cards-list/editing/)) * **Delete** - Remove this card * **Select / Cut** - Enter multi-select mode (see [Multi-Select](/docs/cards-list/multi-select/)) * **Add a new card here** - Insert a new card after this position
### Long-Press a Card **Long-Press a Card** select the card and switch to the multi-select mode. TODO ### Swipe a Card (Left or Right) **Swipe to delete** - Card disappears with undo option See [Deleting Cards](/docs/cards-list/deleting/) for details. *** ## Card Operations The Cards List supports these operations: | Operation | How To | Details | |-----------|--------|---------| | **Popup Menu** | Tap | [Editing Guide](/docs/cards-list/editing/) | | **Reorder** | Long-press & drag | [Reordering Guide](/docs/cards-list/reordering/) | | **Select** | Long-press | [Multi-Select Guide](/docs/cards-list/multi-select/) | In the selection mode: | Operation | How To | Details | |-----------|--------|---------| | **Select** next card | Tap | [Editing Guide](/docs/cards-list/editing/) | | **Popup Menu** | Long-press | [Reordering Guide](/docs/cards-list/reordering/) | *** ## Related Topics * [Editing Cards](/docs/cards-list/editing/) - Modify card content * [Reordering Cards](/docs/cards-list/reordering/) - Drag and drop to change sequence * [Deleting Cards](/docs/cards-list/deleting/) - Remove cards with undo * [Multi-Select Operations](/docs/cards-list/multi-select/) - Bulk actions * [Formatting Cards](/docs/cards-list/formatting/) - Use HTML for rich content * [Study Mode](/docs/study/) - Learn your cards * [Browse Mode](/docs/browse/) - View cards without studying

title: Multi-Select Cards
description: Select multiple flashcards at once for bulk operations like moving, cutting, pasting, and deleting.
content:
## Entering Multi-Select Mode **Method 1: Card Menu** 1. Tap a card 2. Select **"Select / Cut"** from the menu **Method 2: Long-Press** * Long-press any card to instantly enter multi-select mode ## Working with Selected Cards ### Selecting and Deselecting * **Tap any card** to add it to your selection * **Tap a selected card** again to deselect it * Selected cards will be visually highlighted ### Multi-Select Actions Menu While in multi-select mode, **long-press any card** to open the action menu: * **Select** – Select subsequent cards in the list * **Delete selected** – Permanently remove all selected cards * **Paste** – Move all selected cards to appear after the long-pressed card
### Exiting Multi-Select Mode **Tap the back arrow (←)** in the toolbar to deselect all cards and return to normal browsing mode. ## Important Limitations ⚠️ **Deck-Specific Operations** – Cut and paste operations only work within the same deck. You cannot cut cards from one deck and paste them into a different deck. ## Related Topics * [Cards List](/docs/cards-list/) – Overview of card management * [Reordering Cards](/docs/cards-list/reordering/) – Single card repositioning * [Deleting Cards](/docs/cards-list/deleting/) – Single and bulk deletion * [Export/Import Database](/docs/home/database-backup/) – Move entire decks between devices

title: Reordering Cards
description: GoCards supports intuitive drag-and-drop reordering in the Cards List, allowing you to arrange your flashcards in any order you prefer.
content:
## How to Reorder Cards

### Drag-and-Drop Method

1. **Long-press a card** (hold for approximately 1 second)
2. **Drag the card** up or down to your desired position
3. **Release** to drop the card in place
4. The card slides into its new position, and all other cards automatically renumber

### Multi-Select Cut and Paste Method

1. **Long-press a card** (hold for approximately 1 second) to enter multi-select mode
2. **Tap additional cards** to add them to your selection
3. **Long-press the card** after which you want to move your selected cards
4. The multi-select action menu appears
5. **Tap "Paste"**
6. Selected cards are moved to the new location

For more details, see [Multi-Select Operations](/docs/cards-list/multi-select/).

<center>
  <iframe width="395" height="700" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/vp0MnQeySlo?start=60" title="YouTube video player" frameBorder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerPolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowFullScreen />
</center>

***

## Related Topics

* [Cards List](/docs/cards-list/) – Overview of card management
* [Multi-Select Operations](/docs/cards-list/multi-select/) – Move multiple cards at once
* [Sync Cards](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards/) – Reorder in Excel and sync
* [Study Mode](/docs/study/) – See how card order affects study

title: Cards List - Managing Your Flashcards
description: The Cards List screen is your deck management hub. View all cards at once, search, reorder, edit, delete.
content:
## Searching Cards

### How to Search

1. **Tap the 🔍 search icon** (top-right)
2. **Type your query** in the search field
3. **Results filter instantly** as you type
4. **Tap X** to clear search

<center>
  <iframe width="395" height="700" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/vp0MnQeySlo?start=72" title="YouTube video player" frameBorder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerPolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowFullScreen />
</center>

### Search Features

* **Searches both** term and definition
* **Case-insensitive** - "DNA" matches "dna"
* **Partial matches** - "proto" matches "protocol" and "protein"
* **Real-time** - Updates as you type
* **Highlights matches**

title: Creating a Deck
description: Organize related flashcards into decks.
content: 1. **Tap the "+" button** (top-right, available in Recent Decks or All Decks tabs) 2. **Enter deck name** (e.g., "Spanish Verbs", "Biology Chapter 3") 3. **Tap "Create"** The deck appears in your All Decks list and will show in Recent Decks once you use it.

title: Creating a Folder (Premium feature)
description: Organize related decks into folders.
content: 1. **Go to "All Decks" tab** 2. **Tap "⋮" menu** (top-right) 3. **Select "New Folder"** 4. **Enter folder name** (e.g., "Spanish", "Medical School", "Work Training") 5. **Tap "Create"** You can create folders inside folders for hierarchical organization.

title: Database Backup & Restore
description: Database backups preserve everything all decks, cards, settings, and review history.
content:
## Why Back Up Your Database? * **Complete backup** - Saves all decks, progress, and settings in one file * **Device transfers** - Move your data to a new phone seamlessly * **Safety net** - Protect your data before syncing with Excel/CSV or making major changes *** ## Exporting Your Database **To export a database:** 1. Go to the **home screen** (Recent Decks or All Decks tab) 2. Tap **deck** → **More (⋮)** → **Export DB** 3. Choose save location ### What's Included in the Export The exported `.db` file contains: * ✅ All cards in the deck * ✅ Card content (terms and definitions) * ✅ Learning progress (review intervals, memorization status) * ✅ Deck settings and preferences *** ## Importing a Database **To import a database:** 1. Go to the **home screen** (Recent Decks or All Decks tab) 2. Tap the **"⋮" menu** (top-right corner) -→ **"Import DB"** 3. Choose the `.db` file from your device 4. Decks appear in All Decks list
*** ## Best Practices ### Recommended Storage Locations **Cloud storage (recommended):** * **Google Drive** - Automatic cloud backup with version history * **Dropbox** - Cross-device sync and easy sharing * **OneDrive** - Integrated with Microsoft accounts **Local storage:** * **Downloads folder** - Then manually transfer to your computer * **Email to yourself** - Quick option for small databases *** ## Database vs. Excel/CSV Export Choose the right export format for your needs: | Feature | Database Export (.db) | Deck Export (Excel/CSV) | |---------|----------------------|-------------------------| | **All cards** | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes | | **Learning progress** | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | | **Review intervals** | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | | **Deck settings** | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | | **Term/Definition ratio** | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | | **Font size settings** | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | | **Editable on computer** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes | | **Sync with file** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes | ### When to Use Database Export * Creating complete backups with all progress * Transferring data to a new device * Taking snapshots before major changes * Preserving your learning history ### When to Use Excel/CSV Export * Editing cards in Excel or Google Sheets * Sharing deck content with others (without progress) * Syncing cards between devices using files * Bulk editing card content *** ## Related Topics * [Import Cards from Excel/CSV](/docs/sync-export-import/import-cards/) - Add cards from spreadsheets * [Export Cards to Excel/CSV](/docs/sync-export-import/export-cards/) - Save cards for editing * [Sync Cards with Files](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards/) - Keep cards in sync with Excel/CSV

title: 📁 All Decks
description: Your complete deck library with folder organization. Navigate through folders and subfolders to find any deck.
content:
## Topbar Actions On the topbar, you can see the following actions: * **🔍 Search** (Premium) - Find decks by name across all folders * **"+" button** to create a new deck * Tap "⋮" in the topbar in the top-right to open the menu with these options: * New Folder (Premium) * Import Excel * Import CSV * Import DB * Settings
*** ## Deck Menu Actions **Tap "⋮" on the deck** to open the menu with these options: * **Browse** (Premium) - View-only carousel (no grading) * **List Cards** - See all cards in a list * **Add a new card** - Create a new card quickly at the end * **Cut** - Move deck to a different folder * **Rename** - Change the deck name * **Delete** - Remove deck and all its cards (with confirmation) * Tap **More** in the deck menu to access: * **Sync with Excel/CSV** - Connect to an existing Excel/CSV file * **Export to new Excel** - Save deck as .xlsx * **Export to new CSV** - Save deck as .csv * **Export DB** - Backup deck as .db file * **Deck Settings** - Configure auto-sync, forgotten card limits, etc.
*** ## Folder Menu Actions **Tap "⋮" on the folder** to open the menu with these options: * **Rename** - Change folder name * **Delete** - Remove folder with all desks (with confirmation) * **Cut** - Move folder to a different location
*** ## Next Steps * Learn about [Study Mode](/docs/study/) to start learning * Explore [Cards List](/docs/cards-list/) to manage your cards * Set up [File Sync](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards/) to edit on your computer (Premium)

title: Moving Decks Between Folders
description: Move flashcard decks between folders using cut and paste in GoCards.
content: To reorganize your deck structure: 1. **Open the "All Decks" tab** (cut is not available in Recent Decks) 2. **Tap "⋮" menu** on the deck or folder you want to move 3. **Tap "Cut"** — a paste toolbar appears at the bottom 4. **Navigate to the destination folder** 5. **Tap "Paste the deck here"** in the toolbar The deck moves to the new location. The same process works for moving folders to different locations.

title: Moving Decks Between Folders
description: Move flashcard decks between folders using cut and paste in GoCards.
content: To reorganize your deck structure: 1. **Open the "All Decks" tab** (cut is not available in Recent Decks) 2. **Tap "⋮" menu** on the deck or folder you want to move 3. **Tap "Cut"** — a paste toolbar appears at the bottom 4. **Navigate to the destination folder** 5. **Tap "Paste the deck here"** in the toolbar The deck moves to the new location. The same process works for moving folders to different locations.

title: Searching Decks
description: Learn how to search for flashcard decks by name across all folders in GoCards.
content: In the **All Decks** tab: 1. **Tap the 🔍 search icon** (top-right) 2. **Type deck name** (search is case-insensitive) 3. **Results appear instantly** as you type 4. **Tap a result** to open that deck's menu Search looks through deck names across all folders.

title: Formatting Cards with HTML
description: GoCards supports basic HTML formatting in both terms and definitions, letting you create rich, visually appealing flashcards with bold text, images, lists, and more.
content:
## Supported HTML Tags | HTML Example | Description | |--------------|-------------| | **Text Formatting** | | `Important` | **Bold** text | | `Key concept` | **Bold** text (semantic) | | `Emphasis` | *Italic* text | | `Foreign word` | *Italic* text (semantic) | | `Underlined` | Underline text | | `Line 1
Line 2` | Line break | | **Headings** | | `

Chapter 1

` | Main heading (largest) | | `

Section 1.1

` | Subheading | | `

Subsection

` | Smaller heading | | **Lists** | | `
  • Apple
  • Banana
` | Unordered list (bullets) | | `
  1. First
  2. Second
` | Ordered list (numbers) | | **Images** | | `` | Display image from URL | | `` | Image with custom width (in pixels) | | `` | Image with alt text for accessibility | | **Links** | | `Click here` | Clickable link with custom text | | `https://example.com` | Plain URL (automatically converted to clickable link) | *** ## Automatic Link Conversion **You don't need to use HTML tags for links!** GoCards automatically converts plain URLs into clickable links. Simply type or paste a URL directly into your card: ``` https://example.com www.example.com ``` Both will become clickable links in study and browse modes. Use the `` tag only when you want custom link text like "Click here" instead of showing the full URL. ***

title: Editing Cards
description: Modify your flashcards' terms, definitions, and formatting anytime without affecting your learning progress.
content:
## How to Access the Edit Screen

You can edit cards from three locations:

**1. From Cards List**

Tap any card and "Edit" from the popup menu.

**2. From Study Mode**

Tap the "Edit" from the top app bar

**3. From Browse Mode** (Premium feature)

Tap the "Edit" from the top app bar

***

## Topbar Actions

On the topbar, you can see the following actions:

* **💾 Save** - Save your changes to the card
* **🗑️ Delete** - Remove this card from your deck
* **⋮ More menu:**
  * Create new card - Add another card to this deck

**Note:** Your learning progress (review history, due dates, intervals) remains unchanged when you edit a card.

***

## Related Topics

* [Formatting Cards](/docs/cards-list/formatting/) - Add bold, italics, images, and more with HTML
* [Creating Cards](/docs/cards-list/creating/) - Add new flashcards to your decks
* [Deleting Cards](/docs/cards-list/deleting/) - Remove cards you no longer need
* [Sync Cards](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards/) - Edit in Excel and sync back (Premium)
* [Study Mode](/docs/study/) - See how your edited cards appear during review

title: Getting Started with GoCards
description: Quick start guide to help you create your first deck, add cards, and begin studying with GoCards flashcard app.
content:
{/*
  IMPROVEMENT SUGGESTIONS:
  1. Add screenshot: Home screen showing 4 tabs
  2. Add screenshot: Creating a new deck (+ button and dialog)
  3. Add screenshot: Adding first card (Term/Definition fields)
  4. Add screenshot: Study mode with grading buttons
  */}

Welcome to GoCards! This guide will walk you through creating your first deck and starting your learning journey.

## Your First Time Opening GoCards

When you open GoCards, you'll see the home screen with **4 tabs** at the bottom:

* **Recent Decks** (⏱️) - Shows your recently accessed decks
* **All Decks** (📊) - Browse all decks and folders
* **Explore** (🌐) - Community features (coming soon)
* **Discover** (📰) - Premium features and updates

Let's start in the **All Decks** tab.

## Step 1: Create Your First Deck

1. **Tap the "All Decks" tab** at the bottom
2. **Tap the "+" button** in the bottom-right corner
3. **Enter a name** for your deck (e.g., "Spanish Vocabulary", "Biology Terms")
4. **Tap "Create"**

Your new deck now appears in the list!

## Step 2: Add Your First Cards

1. **Tap on your deck** to open the deck menu
2. **Select "List Cards"**
3. **Tap the "+" button** in the top-right
4. **Enter the Term** (the question - front of the card)
5. **Enter the Definition** (the answer - back of the card)
6. **Tap the save icon** (💾)

Repeat to add more cards. We recommend starting with at least 5-10 cards.

### Quick Tips for Creating Cards

* Keep both term and definition concise
* One concept per card works best
* Don't worry about order - you can reorder them later by dragging

## Step 3: Study Your Cards

Now it's time to learn!

1. **Go back** to your deck (tap the back arrow)
2. **Tap on the deck** again
3. **Select "Study Cards"**

### Your First Study Session

The study screen shows:

* **Term** at the top (the question)
* **Tap the bottom section** to reveal the definition (answer)
* **Swipe left or right** to navigate between cards

After revealing the answer, **grade yourself** with one of four buttons:

| Button | When to Use | Next Review |
|--------|-------------|-------------|
| **Again** (Red) | You forgot | Soon (stays in current session) |
| **Quick** (Orange) | For cramming | 5 minutes |
| **Hard** (Blue) | Remembered with difficulty | 3 days |
| **Easy** (Green) | Remembered easily | 5 days |

Each button shows when you'll see the card again. The app uses **spaced repetition** to help you learn efficiently.

## Step 4: Explore More Features

You're now ready to use GoCards! Here are some next steps:

### Organize with Folders (Premium)

Create folders in the "All Decks" tab to group related decks.

### Browse Your Cards

Want to review cards without studying? Use "Browse Cards" from the deck menu for a view-only carousel.

### Customize Your Experience

* **Pinch to zoom** in study mode to adjust text size
* **Dark mode** available in App Settings
* **Edge bars** show card status (learning progress, sync status)

### Sync with Excel/CSV (Premium)

Edit cards on your computer and automatically sync them to your phone - without losing study progress!

## Need Help?

* Check our [FAQ](/docs/support/faq/) for common questions
* Visit [Troubleshooting](/docs/support/troubleshooting/) if you encounter issues
* Browse [Tips & Best Practices](/docs/support/tips/) to optimize your learning

Happy studying! 🎓

title: Home Screen
description: The GoCards home screen is your command center for managing all your flashcard decks.
content:
### 📊 Tab 1: Recent Decks Shows decks you've recently accessed, ordered by last use. This tab helps you quickly jump back into your active learning. ### 📁 Tab 2: All Decks Your complete deck library with folder organization. Navigate through folders and subfolders to find any deck. ### 🌐 Tab 3: Explore Community deck sharing (coming soon) ### 📰 Tab 4: Discover Premium offers, updates, and community engagement. **Features:** * Premium feature highlights * Feedback and suggestions * App updates and news
*** ## Next Steps * Learn about [Study Mode](/docs/study/) to start learning * Explore [Cards List](/docs/cards-list/) to manage your cards * Set up [File Sync](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards/) to edit on your computer (Premium)

title: Overview
description: Welcome to the ultimate GoCards guide! I'll take you on a tour of the app, discovering features you may not have explored yet!
content:
<center>
  <iframe width="395" height="700" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/vp0MnQeySlo?si=QhQDOGJ2anKaqTQV" title="YouTube video player" frameBorder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerPolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowFullScreen />
</center>

## Study mode

Let's start by creating a sample deck and exploring the study mode. Navigating between cards is as simple as sliding them left or right.

At the top, you will find the term, representing the front of the card.
Take a moment to check if you still remember this card.
Then when you are ready, click the bottom part to reveal the definition, representing the back of the card.
Below are buttons that allow you to assess how well you know the card.

### “Again” and the limit forgotten cards

If you hit “Again”, the card stays in the stack and pops up again after studying and forgetting another 5 cards.
You have the flexibility to customize this value or disable the feature in the Deck Settings.

### “Quick repetition”

The next button “Quick repetition” is designed for last-minute cramming e.g. before tomorrow's exam.
Tap it and the card reappears quite quickly, first after 5 minutes, then 10 minutes, followed by 20 minutes, and so on.

### “Hard” and “Easy”

Depending on how well you are confident with the material, use the 'Hard' or 'Easy' button.
“Hard” brings the card back in 3 days, with subsequent intervals increasing the days by 1.5 times.
“Easy” brings the card back in 5 days, with subsequent doubling days.

### Gestures

GoCards prioritizes an interactive user experience by avoiding complicated configuration options in the settings.
Instead, it offers intuitive gestures for adjusting display settings.
You can change the aspect ratio of terms and definitions by moving a middle bar.
You can also adjust text size by zooming in or out.
Display settings can be easily reset to their default setup using the "Reset view" menu option.

### Actions

In the study mode you can also delete cards, edit cards and add create a new card.

#### Undo

Have you accidentally pressed the button or removed the card?
Don’t worry, you can easily undo the last action by pressing the "Back" button. Let’s tap the rating button.
Changed your mind? Just press “Back”. Let’s add a new card, now undo it.
Removed a card you didn't mean to? Let’s bring it back.
To exit the study mode, simply tap the back arrow located in the top toolbar.
Now, let's move on to the next activity with a list of all the cards.

## Cards list

The cards list offers a variety of features to help you manage your flashcards:

* Search for cards using text
* Rearrange the order of cards by dragging and dropping
* Remove cards by swapping them away
* Select multiple cards and paste them elsewhere or delete them
* Edit existing cards
* Add new cards anywhere you like

title: Default Deck Settings
description: Configure default settings that apply to all decks unless overridden by deck-specific custom settings.
content:
## Available Settings

## Studying

### Limit Forgotten Cards

**What it does:** Controls how many "Again" cards can accumulate before reviewing them.

**How it works:**

* When you mark a card as "Again", it remains in the current session queue
* Once you reach the limit (default: 5 cards), the session loops back to review the first forgotten card
* This prevents you from progressing through all cards without reinforcing the ones you're struggling with

***

## Card List

### Max Lines

**What it does:** Limits the number of text lines displayed per card in list view.

**Note:** Study mode always shows the full card content—this setting only affects the card list view.

***

### Show Left Edge Bar

**What it does:** Displays a colored status indicator along the left edge of cards in list view.

**Options:**

* **Off** - No indicator shown
* **Show learning status** - Displays card memorization status
* **Show recently synced** - Displays recent sync activity

Read more in [Edge Bards](/docs/cards-list/edge-bars).

***

### Show Right Edge Bar

**What it does:** Displays a colored status indicator along the right edge of cards in list view.

**Options:**

* **Off** - No indicator shown
* **Show learning status** - Displays card memorization status
* **Show recently synced** - Displays recent sync activity

Read more in [Edge Bards](/docs/cards-list/edge-bars).

***

## How to Access

\*\*Method 1: \*\* Tap a deck → **"More"** → **"Deck Settings"** (gear icon)

\*\*Method 2: **"List Cards"** → **"⋮" menu** → **"Settings"** (gear icon)

This opens the Settings screen on the **"All Decks"** tab.

***

## Related Topics

* [App Settings](/docs/settings/app-settings/) - Global application preferences
* [Study Mode](/docs/study/) - How settings affect your study sessions
* [Sync Cards](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards/) - Card synchronization details

title: App Settings
description: App Settings control global preferences that apply across all decks and the entire app.
content:
## Available Settings

### Dark Mode

**What it does:** Changes app color scheme.

**Options:**

* **Auto** (Default) - Follows Android system dark mode setting
* **Light** - Always light theme
* **Dark** - Always dark theme

***

## How to Access

**Home screen** → **"⋮" menu** (top-right) → **"Settings"**

Opens Settings screen with 3 tabs. This page covers tabs 2 and 3.

## Related Topics

* [Deck Settings](/docs/settings/deck-settings/) - Per-deck configuration
* [Settings Overview](/docs/settings/) - Understanding settings hierarchy
* [Cards List](/docs/cards-list/) - See edge bars in action
* [Study Mode](/docs/study/) - How forgotten card limit affects study

title: Deck Settings
description: Deck Settings let you configure options for a specific deck without affecting other decks.
content:
"This Deck" settings override "All Decks" defaults:

**Example:**

* **All Decks:** Limit forgotten cards = 10
* **This Deck:** Limit forgotten cards = 5
* **Result:** This deck uses 5 (deck setting wins)

**Leave blank/default:** Deck inherits from "All Decks" settings.

***

## Available Settings

### Auto-Sync (Premium feature)

**What it does:** Automatically syncs deck with linked Excel/CSV file when opening or closing the deck.

**Options:**

* ON - Auto-sync enabled
* OFF

See [Sync Cards](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards/) for details.

***

## Studying

### Limit Forgotten Cards

**What it does:** Controls how many "Again" cards can accumulate before reviewing them.

**How it works:**

* When you mark a card as "Again", it remains in the current session queue
* Once you reach the limit (default: 5 cards), the session loops back to review the first forgotten card
* This prevents you from progressing through all cards without reinforcing the ones you're struggling with

***

## Card List

### Max Lines

**What it does:** Limits the number of text lines displayed per card in list view.

**Note:** Study mode always shows the full card content—this setting only affects the card list view.

***

## How to Access

1. **Tap on a deck** → **"More"** → **"Deck Settings"**
2. Or **"List Cards"** → **"⋮" menu** → **"Deck Settings"** (gear icon)
   Opens Settings screen on **"This Deck" tab**.

## Related Topics

* [App Settings](/docs/settings/app-settings/) - Global and all-decks defaults
* [Study Mode](/docs/study/) - How settings affect study sessions
* [Sync Cards](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards/) - Auto-sync details

title: Settings Overview
description: Learn how to configure GoCards app settings, deck-specific settings, and global preferences.
content:
{/*
  IMPROVEMENT SUGGESTIONS:
  1. Add screenshot: Settings screen showing 3 tabs
  2. Add screenshot: Each settings tab (This Deck, All Decks, App)
  3. Add diagram: Settings hierarchy flowchart (how deck overrides work)
  */}

GoCards offers flexible settings at three levels: deck-specific, all-decks, and app-wide. Access settings through the deck menu or app menu.

## How to Access Settings

### Deck Settings

1. **Tap on a deck** → **"More"** → **"Deck Settings"**
2. Or from **"List Cards"** → **"⋮" menu** → **"Deck Settings"** (gear icon)

### App Settings

1. **Home screen** → **"⋮" menu** (top-right) → **"App Settings"**

## Settings Organization

The Settings screen has **3 tabs:**

### Tab 1: This Deck

Settings for the current deck only. See [Deck Settings](/docs/settings/deck-settings/).

### Tab 2: All Decks

Default settings applied to all decks. See [App Settings](/docs/settings/app-settings/) - All Decks section.

### Tab 3: App

Global app preferences (dark mode, edge bars). See [App Settings](/docs/settings/app-settings/).

## Settings Hierarchy

**Priority order:**

1. **Deck-specific settings** (highest priority)
2. **All Decks defaults**
3. **App settings** (global)

**Example:** If "Limit forgotten cards" is set to 10 in "All Decks" but 5 in "This Deck", the deck uses 5.

## Common Settings Tasks

### Change Dark Mode

**App tab** → **Dark Mode** → Choose: Auto / Light / Dark

### Adjust Study Session Length

**This Deck tab** → **Limit forgotten cards** → Set number (e.g., 5, 10, 20)

### Enable Auto-Sync (Premium)

**This Deck tab** → **Auto-Sync** → Toggle ON

### Hide Edge Bars

**All Decks tab** → **Show left edge bar** → Toggle OFF
**All Decks tab** → **Show right edge bar** → Toggle OFF

### Change Card Display Lines

**This Deck tab** → **Max lines** → Set number (1-10)

## Related Topics

* [Deck Settings](/docs/settings/deck-settings/) - Per-deck configuration
* [App Settings](/docs/settings/app-settings/) - Global preferences

title: Study Mode - Learning with Spaced Repetition
description: Master your flashcards with GoCards' study mode featuring spaced repetition algorithm, four grading buttons, interactive gestures, and progress tracking.
content:
<center>
  <iframe width="395" height="700" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/vp0MnQeySlo?si=QhQDOGJ2anKaqTQV&t=20" title="YouTube video player" frameBorder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerPolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowFullScreen />
</center>

## Auto-Sync Feature (Premium)

If you've set up [File Sync](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards/):

**When study session starts:**

* Automatically syncs with your Excel/CSV file
* Imports any changes from the file
* Updates GoCards with latest card edits

**When study session ends:**

* Syncs progress back to file (if file format supports it)
* Keeps everything up to date

Enable in [Deck Settings](/docs/settings/deck-settings/) → "Auto-Sync"

title: Interactive Gestures
description: Study mode is highly interactive. No need to dig through settings!
content:
<center>
  <iframe width="395" height="700" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/vp0MnQeySlo?si=QhQDOGJ2anKaqTQV&t=20" title="YouTube video player" frameBorder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerPolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowFullScreen />
</center>

## Pinch to Zoom

**Gesture:** Pinch out (spread fingers) or pinch in on any card section

**What it does:**

* Increases or decreases text size
* Applies to both term and definition
* Zoom level is saved per deck

**Great for:**

* Reading small text
* Fitting more content on screen
* Accessibility needs

## Adjust Term/Definition Ratio

**Gesture:** Drag the horizontal bar between term and definition

**What it does:**

* Makes term section larger/smaller
* Makes definition section smaller/larger
* Ratio is saved per deck

**Useful when:**

* Your terms are short but definitions are long (or vice versa)
* You want to see more of one section
* Different decks have different content lengths

## Reset View

**How:** Tap "⋮" menu → "Reset View"

**What it does:**

* Restores default term/definition ratio
* Resets text zoom to default
* Quick way to undo accidental gestures

title: Study Mode - Learning with Spaced Repetition
description: Master your flashcards with GoCards' study mode featuring spaced repetition algorithm, four grading buttons, interactive gestures, and progress tracking.
content:
## The Four Grading Buttons

After revealing the answer, grade how well you remembered:

### 🔴 Again (Red)

**Use when:** You forgot the card completely

**What happens:**

* Card stays in the current study session
* Reappears after you've studied/forgotten 5 more cards
* Next review: **2 days** (or current interval if already learned)
* Marks card as "forgotten" (visible in card list edge bar)

**Customization:** Change the "forgotten card limit" in [Deck Settings](/docs/settings/deck-settings/)

***

### 🟠 Quick Repetition (Orange)

**Use when:** Cramming for an exam or need short-term recall

**What happens:**

* First review: **5 minutes**
* Second review: **10 minutes**
* Third review: **20 minutes**
* Intervals keep doubling

**Perfect for:**

* Last-minute studying before exams
* Memorizing information needed in the next few hours
* Intensive drilling of difficult material

***

### 🔵 Hard (Blue)

**Use when:** You remembered, but it took effort

**What happens:**

If this is your **first time** grading the card:

* Next review: **3 days**
* Subsequent reviews: **interval × 1.5**

If you previously clicked **"Again"**:

* Updates the failed attempt with **interval × 0.5**
* Creates new review with **interval × 1.5**

**Example progression:**

1. First: 3 days
2. Second: 4.5 days (3 × 1.5)
3. Third: 6.75 days (4.5 × 1.5)

***

### 🟢 Easy (Green)

**Use when:** You remembered quickly and confidently

**What happens:**

If this is your **first time** grading the card:

* Next review: **5 days**
* Subsequent reviews: **interval × 2**

If you previously clicked **"Again"**:

* Updates the failed attempt with **interval × 0.5**
* Marks card as memorized

**Example progression:**

1. First: 5 days
2. Second: 10 days (5 × 2)
3. Third: 20 days (10 × 2)
4. Fourth: 40 days (20 × 2)

***

## Understanding Review Intervals

The interval shown below each button is **when you'll see this card again**.

### How Intervals Work

**New cards** (never studied):

* Again: 2 days
* Quick: 5 minutes
* Hard: 3 days
* Easy: 5 days

**Previously studied cards:**

* Intervals adapt based on your history
* More "Easy" clicks = longer intervals
* "Again" clicks = shorter intervals
* The algorithm balances memory retention with study efficiency

### Why These Intervals?

GoCards uses **spaced repetition** based on memory research:

* Review just before you're about to forget
* Longer intervals strengthen long-term memory
* Too frequent = waste of time
* Too infrequent = you'll forget

## Related Topics

* [Browse Mode](/docs/browse/) - View cards without studying
* [Cards List](/docs/cards-list/) - See all cards and their status
* [Deck Settings](/docs/settings/deck-settings/) - Customize study behavior
* [Tips & Best Practices](/docs/support/tips/) - Optimize your learning

title: Study Mode - Learning with Spaced Repetition
description: Master your flashcards with GoCards' study mode featuring spaced repetition algorithm, four grading buttons, interactive gestures, and progress tracking.
content:
## Starting a Study Session

GoCards automatically selects which cards to show based on:

* Cards you've never seen before
* Cards due for review today
* Cards you marked as "Again" recently

***

## Navigation

* **Swipe left/right** - Move between cards
* **Tap bottom section** - Reveal the definition (answer) and grading buttons

***

## Card Layout

```
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│    [Toolbar: ↶ + ✏️ 🗑️ ⋮]   │
├─────────────────────────────┤
│                             │
│        TERM (Question)      │ ← Top section (always visible)
│                             │
├─────────────────────────────┤ ← Drag this bar to resize
│                             │
│     DEFINITION (Answer)     │ ← Bottom section (tap to reveal)
│                             │
├─────────────────────────────┤
│  [Again] [Quick] [Hard] [Easy] │ ← Grading buttons
│   2 days   5 min   3 days  5 days│ ← Next review time
└─────────────────────────────┘
```

***

## Toolbar Options (Top)

* [**✏️ Edit Card**](/docs/cards-list/editing/)
* **⋮ More Menu**
  * **+ New** - Create new card
  * **🗑️ Delete** - Removes the current card (with confirmation)
  * **(TODO insert undo icon) Reset view** - resets view settings like zoom, size, ratio betwen term and definition

***

### Adjust Settings

* Increase forgotten card limit if sessions are too short
* See [Deck Settings](/docs/settings/deck-settings/)

***

## Related Topics

* [Browse Mode](/docs/browse/) - View cards without studying
* [Cards List](/docs/cards-list/) - See all cards and their status
* [Deck Settings](/docs/settings/deck-settings/) - Customize study behavior
* [Tips & Best Practices](/docs/support/tips/) - Optimize your learning

title: Undo last action
description: Use the ← button to undo your last action like grading a card, deleting a card, or entering edit card.
content:
The ← (back button) has two different functions depending on where you tap it:

**1. Exit current activity** (top bar)

* **Tap ←** in the top bar to close your current activity and return to the Deck List

**2. Undo last action** (bottom navigation bar)

* **Tap ←** in the bottom navigation bar to reverse your most recent operation

### **What you can undo:**

When you tap ← in the bottom navigation bar, you can:

* **Undo grading** – Return to the previous card after selecting Easy, Good, Hard, or Again
* **Restore deleted card** – Recover a card you just removed
* **Exit Edit Card mode** – Return to your previous activity (Study or Browse mode)

### **See it in action:**

<center>
  <iframe width="395" height="700" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/vp0MnQeySlo?si=QhQDOGJ2anKaqTQV&start=40" title="YouTube video player" frameBorder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerPolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowFullScreen />
</center>

title: Export Cards to Excel/CSV
description: Learn how to export your GoCards flashcard decks to Excel (.xlsx) or CSV files for editing on your computer, sharing with others, or backup purposes.
content:
Export your flashcard decks to Excel (.xlsx) or CSV (.csv) files to edit on your computer, share with others, or create backups.

1. **From home screen**, **tap on a deck** to open menu
2. **Select "More" (⋮)**
3. **Choose "Export to Excel"** or **"Export to CSV"**
4. **Choose save location**
5. **File is saved**

***

## Related Topics

* [Import Cards](/docs/sync-export-import/import-cards/) - Import Excel/CSV files
* [Sync Cards](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards/) - Bi-directional auto-sync (Premium)
* [File Format Guide](/docs/sync-export-import/file-format/) - Detailed file structure specs
* [Cloud Storage Setup](/docs/sync-export-import/cloud-storage/) - Use with Google Drive/Dropbox
* [Database Backup](/docs/home/database-backup/) - Full backup including progress

title: File Format Specifications
description: Detailed specifications for creating Excel (.xlsx) and CSV (.csv) files compatible with GoCards.
content:
## Required File Structure

You can also simply export the deck to see an example of the file format.

| Column | Required | Data Type | Description |
|--------|----------|-----------|-------------|
| **Term** | ✅ Required | Text | Question/prompt (front of card) |
| **Definition** | ✅ Required | Text | Answer (back of card) |
| **Disabled** | ❌ Optional | Boolean | TRUE/FALSE - card enabled status |

**Important:**

* Order doesn't matter.

## CSV Format (.csv)

```csv
Term,Definition,Disabled
"What is DNA?","Deoxyribonucleic acid",FALSE
"Capital of France?","Paris",FALSE
```

## Related Topics

* [Import Cards](/docs/sync-export-import/import-cards/) - How to import files
* [Export Cards](/docs/sync-export-import/export-cards/) - How to export files
* [Sync Cards](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards/) - How syncing works
* [Troubleshooting](/docs/sync-export-import/troubleshooting/) - Solve problems

title: Import Cards from Excel/CSV
description: Import flashcard decks from Excel (.xlsx) or CSV (.csv) files to quickly create new decks, migrate from other apps, or restore backups.
content:
## How to Import Cards

1. **Go to home screen** (Recent Decks or All Decks tab)
2. **Tap "⋮" menu** (top-right)
3. **Select "Import Excel"** or **"Import CSV"**
4. **Select the file** (Downloads, Google Drive, etc.)
5. **New deck is created** with the filename as deck name

**Before importing:** Make sure your file follows the correct format. See the [File Format Guide](/docs/sync-export-import/file-format/) for specifications.

***

## When to Use Import vs. Sync

| Feature | Import | Sync |
|---------|--------|------|
| **Creates new deck** | ✅ Yes | ❌ No (updates existing) |
| **Preserves learning progress** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |
| **Bi-directional updates** | ❌ No | ✅ Yes |

***

## Related Topics

* [Export Cards](/docs/sync-export-import/export-cards/) - Export decks to Excel/CSV
* [Sync Cards](/docs/sync-export-import/sync-cards/) - Set up ongoing sync instead of one-time import
* [File Format Guide](/docs/sync-export-import/file-format/) - Detailed file structure specifications
* [Cloud Storage](/docs/sync-export-import/cloud-storage/) - Import from Google Drive/Dropbox
* [Troubleshooting](/docs/sync-export-import/troubleshooting/) - Solve import problems

title: Sync, Export & Import
content: ## Documents

title: Sync Cards with Excel/CSV Files
description: Keep your flashcard decks synchronized with Excel or CSV files stored in cloud storage. Edit your cards on a laptop, save to Google Drive or Dropbox, and changes automatically sync to your phone—all while preserving your study progress.
content:
## Why Sync Instead of Import?

**The Problem with Traditional Apps:**

Most flashcard apps only support importing files. Every time you update your flashcards, you have to delete the old deck and import a new one, losing all your study progress in the process.

**The GoCards Solution:**

Sync your deck once, then edit the file anytime. Changes flow automatically in both directions while your learning statistics, due dates, and mastery levels stay intact.

***

## How Sync Works

GoCards uses smart synchronization that compares modification dates to determine which source has the newest changes.

### Intelligent Card Matching

When syncing, GoCards automatically detects whether a card has been modified or is entirely new:

* **Modified cards** - Updated if there's at least 70% similarity in the term or definition (prevents accidental overwrites)
* **New cards** - Added automatically if similarity is below 70%
* **Deleted cards** - Removed if they no longer exist in the source

This smart matching ensures your edits are preserved while preventing duplicate cards.

### What Gets Synced

**When the file is newer than the deck:**

* New cards from the file are added
* Deleted cards are removed
* Modified cards are updated
* Card order matches the file
* Study progress is preserved

**When the card is newer than the file:**

* Changes from the card are written back to the file
* Custom columns in your spreadsheet are preserved and move with their cards

***

## See It in Action

Watch how sync works in practice:

<center>
  <iframe width="395" height="700" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/rthjtSAq13A?si=ipRAJYeRNYG1ermM" title="YouTube video player" frameBorder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerPolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowFullScreen />
</center>

**What happens in this demo:**

1. A deck is exported to create a synced file
2. Changes are made to both the file (3 cards) and the app (3 cards)
3. Sync is triggered
4. The card list shows what changed with color indicators
5. Study progress remains intact despite the updates

***

## Visual Sync Indicators

After syncing, colored edge bars in your card list show exactly which cards changed.

Learn more in [Edge Bars](/docs/cards-list/edge-bars).

***

## Key Benefits

✅ **Preserve Study Progress** - Never lose your learning statistics again

✅ **Bi-directional Sync** - Changes flow both ways automatically

✅ **Edit Anywhere** - Update cards on laptop or phone

✅ **Cloud Storage Integration** - Works with Google Drive, Dropbox, and more

✅ **Smart Conflict Resolution** - Prevents accidental data loss with similarity checking

✅ **Extra Data Preserved** - Keep custom columns in your spreadsheet intact

***

## Supported File Formats

**Excel (.xlsx, .xls)**

* Compatible with Microsoft Excel, Google Sheets, LibreOffice Calc

**CSV (.csv)**

* Universal compatibility with any text editor or spreadsheet program

See the [File Format Guide](/docs/sync-export-import/file-format/) for detailed specifications.

title: Why Use Excel/CSV with GoCards?
description: Excel and CSV files give you powerful ways to work with your flashcards outside the GoCards app. Create cards faster, edit in bulk, share with others, and maintain readable backups.
content:
## Benefits of Using Excel/CSV

* ✅ Edit Cards on Your Computer
  * ✅ **Larger screen** - More comfortable than typing on your phone
  * ✅ **Bulk editing** - Use find-replace to update hundreds of cards instantly
  * ✅ **Edit Anywhere** - Update cards on laptop or phone in any program
  * ✅ **Faster data entry** - Create many cards quickly
  * ✅ **Better organization** - Arrange, categorize, and structure before importing
* ✅ Share Decks Easily
* ✅ Backup Content
  * ✅ **Cloud Storage Integration** - Works with Google Drive, Dropbox, and more

***

## Supports both Excel (.xlsx) and CSV (.csv) formats:

**Excel (.xlsx)**

* Compatible with: Excel, Google Sheets, LibreOffice

**CSV (.csv)**

* Best for: Universal compatibility, simple text editors
